events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the compliance determination directly to the required JSON file. The key issue is that a weld shown on engineered detail is missing, so this cannot pass at rough-complete.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 90,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.93,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Required structural weld is missing compared with the engineered drawing detail.\",
    \"As-installed welded connection does not meet the approved drawing/specification requirements at rough-complete stage.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Hold acceptance of the affected connection and flag it for rework.\",
    \"Verify the weld symbol, size, length, location, base material, and WPS requirements against the approved detail.\",
    \"Install the missing weld using qualified welders and an approved WPS, then perform required visual and specified NDE inspection before closeout.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"AWS D1.1 structural welding work must conform to the contract documents and approved weld details. A weld required by the drawing but absent in the installed assembly is incomplete and non-compliant, with potential loss of intended connection capacity until corrected and inspected.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"AWS D1.1 - Structural Welding Code-Steel: workmanship and acceptance requirements for welded structural connections\",
    \"Approved engineered drawing/detail requiring the weld\"
  ]
}' > /app/answer.json
python3 -m json.tool /app/answer.json"
